channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Pence reveals words exchanged with President-elect Trump at Carter funeral
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence recently revealed the words he exchanged with President-elect Trump at the funeral for late President Carter.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Californians contend with looting, price gouging, insurance issues
item.description: California has implemented a one-year moratorium on insurance companies canceling or refushing to renew policies for homeowners.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: House Republicans face massive debt problem
item.description: House Republicans have a problem. They want to pass a massive agenda for President-elect Trump, preferably in his first 100 days of office. And they don’t want to add to the federal deficit. That looks impossible.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Political experts discuss Trump-Newsom relationship, political fallout for LA Mayor Bass amid wildfires
item.description: (INSIDE CALIFORNIA POLITICS) — The Los Angeles County wildfires have claimed several lives and burned thousands of structures have also caused a stir in the political sphere. San Francisco Chronicle columnist Emily Hoeven spoke with Inside California Politics about how L.A.
item.dc:creator: Jacque Porter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden honors Pope Francis with the Presidential Medal of Freedom
item.description: President Joe Biden has honored Pope Francis with the Presidential Medal of Freedom with distinction.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Congress braces for California wildfire fallout
item.description: House lawmakers are bracing for what could be a contentious battle over emergency spending in the wake of the wildfires that are tearing through Southern California — an unprecedented disaster that’s already estimated to have caused more than $50 billion in damage in and around Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: SALT-focused Republicans to meet with Trump as they threaten to hold up bill
item.description: Republicans who are pledging to withhold support for President-elect Trump’s ambitious agenda if the state and local tax (SALT) deduction cap is not raised are set to meet with the incoming president this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden extends time in US for 800,000 Venezuelans, Salvadorans as Trump readies immigration crackdown
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — About 600,000 Venezuelans and more than 230,000 Salvadorans already living in the United States can legally remain another 18 months, the Department of Homeland Security said Friday, barely a week before President-elect Donald Trump takes office with promises of hardline immigration pol
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dems eerily silent on Trump sentencing as they prepare for Republican trifecta in Washington
item.description: Democratic members of congress were less reactive to the sentencing of President-elect Trump on Friday, where he was sentenced with no penalty in the New York criminal trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: LA Mayor Karen Bass faces critical leadership test as questions emerge about wildfire response
item.description: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass is facing withering criticism for her response to the firestorm ravaging her city.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi And Michael R. Blood, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump vows to shed light on mystery drones when president
item.description: Trump, who will be inaugurated as the 47th president on Jan. 20, criticized the Biden administration's handling of the mystery drones.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to uphold law that would force TikTok to shut down on Jan. 19
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday seemed likely to uphold a law that would ban TikTok in the United States beginning Jan. 19 unless the popular social media program is sold by its China-based parent company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Freedom Caucus members to huddle with Trump on strategy
item.description: Members of the House Freedom Caucus say they will make the case for their preferred policies and strategy when they meet with President-elect Trump at Mar-a-Lago on Friday to discuss his ambitious legislative agenda — bringing preferences that at times clash with those of Trump. Rep.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats fume over Alito-Trump call
item.description: Senate Democrats are furious that President-elect Trump has reached out to conservative Justice Samuel Alito amid a push by Trump for the high court to halt his criminal sentencing.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: U.S. stock markets close to honor former President Jimmy Carter
item.description: The New York Stock Exchange and the Nasdaq will both observe Thursday's national day of mourning in a Wall Street tradition dating back to 1865.
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan, MPR News

item.title: Jimmy Carter remembered by colleagues, family and friends as a devoted public servant
item.description: President Biden delivered a eulogy to former President Jimmy Carter at the National Cathedral service, noting the 39th president's faith and dedication to service.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore, MPR News

item.title: Biden says he could've beaten Trump in November
item.description: “It’s presumptuous to say that, but I think yes,” the president said when reporter Susan Page asked if he could have won.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Senate approves committee assignments, tees up confirmation hearings next week
item.description: The Senate on Tuesday approved its organizing resolution for the 119th Congress, officially setting the composition of committees and greenlighting nomination hearings to take place in the coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Durbin asks DOJ to clarify scope of president’s military power ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.), the top Democrat on the Senate Judiciary Committee, is urging the Department of Justice (DOJ) to rescind a number of internal opinions dealing with a president’s military powers while asking for clarification on domestic use of America’s forces.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: President-elect Trump asks Supreme Court to block sentencing in hush money case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is asking the Supreme Court to call off Friday's sentencing in his hush money case in New York. Trump's lawyers turned to the nation's highest court on Wednesday after New York courts refused to postpone the sentencing by Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump won't commit to not using military to get Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday refused to commit to not using the U.S. military to gain control of the Panama Canal, after vowing last month to take over the operation of the key passageway.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Exxon sues California AG for defamation after he sued them for 'misleading' plastic recycling claims
item.description: A major oil company has responded to California Attorney General Rob Bonta with a lawsuit alleging defamation after he accused them of misleading customers.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, Fox 5

item.title: Curator talks archiving Walz’s bid for VP, preserving political legacies
item.description: While brief, Gov. Tim Walz’s bid for vice president will add another chapter to his legacy in politics, and the Minnesota Historical Society has gotten a jumpstart on preserving it.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Judge grants media requests to record Minnesota senator’s burglary trial
item.description: Minnesota Sen. Nicole Mitchell is set to stand trial later this month on a burglary charge. A Becker County judge on Tuesday ruled that the proceedings can be recorded by media organizations with some limitations.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Wisconsin Republicans pushing to make voter ID a constitutional requirement
item.description: Other states have taken similar steps in recent years to put voter ID requirements in the constitution. Voters approved it in Mississippi in 2011 and North Carolina voters in 2018, while Minnesota voters rejected it in 2012.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: California governor proposes $322B budget with no deficit after two years of shortfalls
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom has proposed a $322 billion budget without a deficit, a welcome change after two years of significant budget shortfalls in the nation's most populous state.
item.dc:creator: Tran Nguyen, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota Office of Cannabis Management will get new interim director
item.description: The interim director of the Minnesota Office of Cannabis Management, who has led the office since it was created, is stepping down.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Canadian PM Justin Trudeau resigns
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation Monday in the face of rising discontent over his leadership, and after the abrupt departure of his finance minister signaled growing turmoil within his government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump lawyers push to halt Jan. 10 hush money case sentencing
item.description: President-elect Trump’s legal team urged a judge to hold off on sentencing him on Jan. 10 on his New York criminal conviction ahead of his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Biden blocks some new offshore drilling ahead of Trump takeover
item.description: President Biden has blocked new offshore drilling for oil and gas in several parts of the country about two weeks before President-elect Trump takes office. Biden has announced that he’ll block new drilling off the entire East Coast, as well as California, Oregon and Washington state.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5
